content.description:
Transclusion embeds content from one item into another inline, without duplicating it. This is Hobson's primary composition mechanism — documentation, dashboards, and live views are all built by transcluding items into markdown.

Implemented by [hobson-markdown](item://a1b2c3d4-5e6f-7a8b-9c0d-e1f2a3b4c5d6). Inserted via the toolbar in [field-view-markdown-editable](item://56f77a00-baf5-43cc-9dc4-8ad0c66f1e8f).

## Syntax

Transclusion uses markdown image syntax with `item://` URLs:

| Syntax | Effect |
|--------|--------|
| `![](item://id)` | Full item transclusion |
| `![](item://id#description)` | Specific field |
| `![](item://id#code?region=name)` | Named region |
| `![](item://id#code?lines=10-20)` | Line range |
| `![](item://id?symbol=name)` | Symbol extraction (auto-resolves to code field) |
| `![](item://id#description?render=code)` | Override render mode |
| `![](item://id?call=fn)` | Functional — call export `fn` |

Full transclusion renders the entire item using `api.renderItem()`. Partial transclusion extracts a specific field, region, symbol, or line range and renders it inline.

## Render Modes

`?render=markdown` or `?render=code` controls how the transcluded text is displayed. By default, `description` fields render as markdown and everything else renders as a code block with line numbers.

## Region Markers

Named regions in code or markdown enable targeted transclusion without brittle line numbers:

```javascript
// [BEGIN:myFunction]
function myFunction() { ... }
// [END:myFunction]
```

Reference with `![](item://id#code?region=myFunction)`. Supported comment styles: `//`, `#`, `<!-- -->`. In rendered markdown, region markers become invisible anchor spans for scroll-to-region navigation.

## Symbol Resolution

`?symbol=name` looks up functions and classes in `content._symbols` (auto-generated metadata). Supports qualified names like `createAPI.get` and falls back to unqualified matching. The symbol's source lines are extracted and displayed as a code block.

## Functional Transclusion

Image syntax with `?call=` loads the target item as a module and calls the named export:

```markdown
![](item://my-lib?call=renderWidget&someParam=value)
```

The function receives `(api, params)` where `params` is the parsed URL query object (excluding `call` itself). It can return:
- A **DOM node** — embedded directly into the document
- A **string** — rendered as markdown

This powers live, interactive content. The [Context Menu](item://c0c0c0c0-0034-0000-0000-000000000000) documentation uses functional transclusion to show the real context menu inline, built from the same code the viewport uses.

## Query Blocks

Fenced code blocks with language `query` execute JavaScript at render time:

````markdown
```query
const items = await api.query({ type: noteTypeId });
return items.map(i => `- [${i.name}](item://${i.id})`).join('\n');
```
````

Query code has access to `api` and two transclusion helpers:

### transclude(item, text, options)

Wraps text with attribution chrome linking back to the source item.

### transcludeEach(items, formatter, options)

Applies `transclude()` to each item using `formatter(item)` for the text.

### Chrome Options

Both helpers accept `{ chrome }` to control attribution display:

| Chrome | Effect |
|--------|--------|
| `'full'` | Styled container with header and collapse toggle |
| `'subtle'` | Small ↗ link marker (default) |
| `'none'` | No wrapper — content appears as if authored in place |

Query blocks can return a DOM node instead of a string. If the result has a `nodeType` property, it is inserted directly without markdown processing.

## Visual Appearance

Transcluded content appears in a collapsible `<details>` box with:
- Light gray background and border
- "Transcluded from: [Item Name] ↗" header
- Clicking the header collapses/expands; clicking ↗ navigates to the source

## Transclusion vs Attachments

| Aspect | Transclusion | [Attachments](item://c0c0c0c0-0005-0000-0000-000000000000) |
|--------|-------------|----------|
| Syntax | `![](item://id)` in markdown | `attachments` array in item |
| Display | Inline within text flow | As positioned windows |
| Relationship | Implicit via content | Explicit in structure |
| Use case | Documentation, composition | Spatial organization |

An item can be both transcluded and attached — these are orthogonal relationships.

## See Also

- [hobson-markdown](item://a1b2c3d4-5e6f-7a8b-9c0d-e1f2a3b4c5d6) — The library that implements transclusion rendering
- [Navigation](item://c0c0c0c0-0003-0000-0000-000000000000) — Link behavior and the `item://` URL scheme
- [Attachments](item://c0c0c0c0-0005-0000-0000-000000000000) — Structural parent-child relationships
- [Context Menu](item://c0c0c0c0-0034-0000-0000-000000000000) — Uses functional transclusion for live documentation
